name: Creamy Chicken Popovers
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine water, chicken, carrot, and sliced onion in a large saucepan.
2. Bring to a boil; cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 30 minutes. Remove the chicken from the broth and cool it down.
3. Bone the chicken and cut into 1-inch pieces. Set aside.
4. Strain the broth, saving 3/4 cup.
5. Coat a large nonstick skillet with cooking spray; place over medium-high heat until hot. Add chopped onion and sauté until tender.
6. Gradually add 1/4 cup flour and tarragon, stirring well. Gradually stir in 3/4 cup skim milk, yogurt, wine, and reserved 3/4 cup chicken broth.
7. Cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture is thickened and bubbly. Stir in peas, pimento, and reserved chicken. Set aside and keep warm.
8. Combine remaining 1 cup flour and salt, stirring well. Add eggs and remaining 1 cup skim milk; beat with a wire whisk until well blended.
9. Spoon the mixture evenly into six 6-ounce custard cups that have been coated with Pam. Place them about 4 inches apart on a baking sheet.
10. Bake at 400 degrees F for 45-50 minutes or until golden brown. Break open each popover and spoon half cup chicken mixture over each popover. Serve immediately.
